channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Romanian court orders recount of the 1st round of the presidential vote, won by a far-right outsider
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Thursday asked the official electoral authority to recount and verify all of the ballots cast in the first round of the presidential election, which was won by a far-right outsider candidate, sending shockwaves through the political establishment.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Avocados, tequila and other iconic Mexican products are jeopardized by Trump's tariff threats
item.description: Mexicans are worried that threats by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump to impose 25% tariffs could affect a wide range of iconic Mexican products, some of which support entire regional economies.
item.dc:creator: Armando Solís, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sweden asks for China's cooperation over Baltic Sea cables cut while a Chinese ship was nearby
item.description: HARPSUND, Sweden (AP) — Sweden has formally asked China to cooperate in explaining the recent rupture of two data cables on the Baltic Sea bed in an area where a China-flagged vessel had been sighted, Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Georgia suspends talks on joining the EU for 4 years, accusing the bloc of blackmail
item.description: Georgia’s prime minister has declared that the South Caucasus country will put negotiations on its bid to join the European Union on hold for four years in view of what he described as “blackmail and manipulation” from some of the bloc’s politicians.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former British soldier who broke out of prison is convicted of spying for Iran
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British soldier whose audacious escape from a London prison spurred a dayslong search was convicted on Thursday of spying for Iran.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin urges Ukraine to lower draft age to conscript 18-year-olds
item.description: The Biden administration is urging Ukraine to lower its draft age to conscript 18-year-olds to keep pace with Russia’s growing military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel launches first airstrike on Lebanon since ceasefire after saying Hezbollah violated the truce
item.description: The Israeli military says its warplanes have fired on southern Lebanon after detecting Hezbollah activity at a rocket storage facility.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia launches another large missile, drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched another attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Thursday, leaving over a million households without power and forcing emergency power to be implemented nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah militants returned home Wednesday as a ceasefire took hold, driving cars stacked with personal belongings and defying warnings from Lebanese and Israeli troops to avoid some areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO chief: Ukraine not in strong position to negotiate with Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said on Wednesday that Ukraine is not in a strong enough position to begin negotiating an end to the war with Russia and said any deal struck must “prevent the Russians from getting what they want.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: 2 companies cut ties with Conor McGregor
item.description: Proximo Spirits, the owner of Irish whiskey brand Proper No. 12, will no longer feature McGregor’s name.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An Australia police officer who shocked a 95-year-old woman with a Taser is guilty of manslaughter
item.description: SYDNEY, Australia (AP) — A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court Wednesday. A jury found Kristian James Samuel White guilty in the trial in Sydney after 20 hours of deliberation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah start a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants began a ceasefire Wednesday in a major step toward ending nearly 14 months of fighting as a region on edge wondered whether it will hold.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you protect children from the harms of social media? Politically, the answer appears simple in Australia, as legislation tackling the problem moves quickly through the Parliament. Practically, however, the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A fugitive wanted in the US for a pair of bombings is arrested in the UK after 20 years on the run
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected animal rights extremist wanted in the U.S. for bombings in the San Francisco area was arrested in Britain after more than 20 years on the run from the law, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Russia tricks Yemeni men to fight in Ukraine under Houthi scheme
item.description: Russia has begun luring Yemeni men to fight in its war in Ukraine by falsely promising lucrative jobs before forcing them to sign military contracts and then sending them to fight on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel moves toward ceasefire deal with Hezbollah: reports
item.description: Israel is reportedly moving toward a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon after nearly a year of fighting escalated into an all-out war
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four men convicted in Spain in connection to 2021 hate crime murder
item.description: Four men were sentenced for their involvement in the homophobic murder of 24-year-old Samuel Luiz, who died at a hospital in July 2021 after being attacked for allegedly being gay.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine to analyze fragments of missile fired by Russia capable of carrying nuclear warheads
item.description: Ukrainian investigators allowed journalists to view remains of an intermediate-range ballistic missile used by Russia on the battlefield, before an analysis could be completed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9
